highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.148 Released — Bash Tool Exit Code Fix
highlights.summary: This patch release fixes a regression introduced in v2.1.147 where the Bash tool incorrectly returned exit code 127 for every command, breaking shell-based workflows. If you noticed your Bash commands failing unexpectedly after updating yesterday, upgrading to v2.1.148 resolves the issue. Always keep Claude Code updated to avoid regressions like this one.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.147 — Pinned Background Sessions & /code-review Command
highlights.summary: This release introduces pinned background sessions (Ctrl+T in `claude agents`) that stay alive when idle and survive Claude Code updates, giving you persistent long-running agents. The `/simplify` command has been renamed to `/code-review`, which now actively reports correctness bugs in your code. These changes make multi-agent workflows and code quality checks much more reliable.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has announced Claude Opus 4.7, the latest addition to the Claude model family with expanded capabilities for complex tasks. Developers using Claude Code can leverage the new model for more demanding coding and reasoning workflows. Check Anthropic's blog for full details on benchmarks and how to access the model in your projects.

highlights.title: Show HN: Spec-Driven Development Workflow for Claude Code
highlights.summary: This approach improves AI coding agent output by breaking work into two dimensions: first generating structured specs (requirements, code analysis, design) in multiple steps, then splitting the implementation into smaller subtasks handled one by one. Using spec-driven development with Claude Code can significantly reduce hallucinations and off-track implementations on large features. It's a practical workflow upgrade worth adopting if you find Claude drifting from your intent on complex tasks.

highlights.title: Learn 80% of Claude Code in 10 Minutes (2026 Tutorial)
highlights.summary: This concise YouTube tutorial aims to get you productive with Claude Code quickly by covering the most essential features in just 10 minutes. It's ideal for developers who want a fast, high-value introduction without sitting through a lengthy course. Bookmark this as a go-to starting point before exploring more advanced topics.

highlights.title: Anthropic Just Revealed The Best Claude Code Setup
highlights.summary: This YouTube video breaks down the optimal configuration and setup for Claude Code as recommended by Anthropic, covering environment, settings, and workflow best practices. Following an Anthropic-endorsed setup helps you avoid common configuration mistakes that slow down your development. Great watch if you want to make sure your Claude Code environment is tuned for peak performance.

highlights.title: Comparing 32 AI Coding Tools: Cursor, Claude Code, Codex, Copilot & More
highlights.summary: This Bilibili video offers hands-on comparisons of 32 AI coding tools including Cursor, Claude Code, GitHub Codex, GitHub Copilot, Windsurf, and others to help you find the best fit for your workflow. Understanding where Claude Code stands relative to alternatives helps you make informed decisions about which tool to use for which task. A useful reference if you're evaluating your AI coding stack in 2026.

tips_of_the_day: Use `/code-review high` (renamed from `/simplify`) after finishing a feature to let Claude scan for correctness bugs — it's now built into Claude Code and requires no extra setup.
If you run long background tasks with Claude Code agents, press Ctrl+T in `claude agents` to pin a session so it stays alive through idle periods and automatic updates without losing your progress.

Before starting a large feature with Claude Code, write a short spec file (requirements + design outline) and pass it as context — this spec-driven approach dramatically reduces the chance of Claude drifting off-target mid-implementation.
